figures.caption: Figure 4 Held-out linear-probe 𝑅2 on hidden velocity (five seeds). UWM-JEPA (0.901 ± 0.032) and LSTM-JEPA (0.894 ± 0.021) are not significantly different (Welch 𝑝 = 0.70). The density-matrix latent is not a win on context probing alone; the orthogonal, GRU, and MLP baselines fall far below.

Figure 3 Blind-rollout evidence. A: top-1 retrieval accuracy for predicted future latents, comparing the UWM unitary predictor to LSTM-JEPA encoders with plain and residual MLP predictors. B: information lost under blind rollout, measured as Δ𝑅2 = 𝑅2 teacher − 𝑅2 blind for frozen probes. Lower is better. The dashed vertical line marks the trained horizon (𝑘 = 5); horizons beyond it are extrapolations, not monotone information-theoretic forgetting curves. The shaded region marks the high-variance weak-teacher regime (𝑘 ≥ 10). Together the two panels show that the context-probe tie in Figure 4 does not translate into matched predictor behaviour.

Figure 2 Action-binding evidence. A: teacher-forced JEPA makes the action Hamiltonian nearly inert, while counterfactual targets recover an action term comparable to the base dynamics. B: action perturbation controls increase Hilbert–Schmidt distance to the counterfactual target. C: the hidden-velocity indicator is solved above chance by UWM-JEPA-CF, while the matched LSTM-JEPA-CF main-comparison configuration collapses to majority-class predictor.

Figure 1 From point latents to belief-structured imagination, and the architecture that realises it. (a) A standard vector-latent JEPA predicts a point in representation space. UWM-JEPA instead represents the latent as a density matrix and rolls it forward on an isospectral orbit. Under partial observability this gives the predictor a structured latent in which uncertainty and hidden modes can be carried through blind rollout; actions steer the unitary trajectory through 𝐻 (𝑎) = 𝐻0 + 𝑎𝐻1. (b) The online encoder maps the current observation history to 𝜌𝑡 ; the predictor applies 𝑈𝑘𝜌𝑡 (𝑈† )𝑘 or an action-conditioned sequence 𝑈 (𝑎𝑡+𝑘−1 ) · · ·𝑈 (𝑎𝑡 )𝜌𝑡𝑈 (𝑎𝑡 )† · · ·𝑈 (𝑎𝑡+𝑘−1 )†. The EMA target encoder supplies the stop-gradient future target, and the JEPA loss is evaluated after the system readout and projection.
